name: Veigar

basic_attack.calculation_notes: Missile speed uses canonical ranged fallback (2000) because source champion payload omits basic-attack missile speed.

passive.name: Phenomenal Evil Power
passive.description_source: Innate:  Veigar generates a  stack of Phenomenal Evil for each enemy  champion hit by his  abilities, and more stacks when he scores an enemy champion  takedown.

effects.description: Innate: Veigar generates a stack of Phenomenal Evil for each enemy champion hit by his abilities, and 5 stacks whenever he scores an enemy champion takedown.
Phenomenal Evil: For each stack, Veigar gains 1 ability power.

passive.description: Innate: Veigar generates a stack of Phenomenal Evil for each enemy champion hit by his abilities, and 5 stacks whenever he scores an enemy champion takedown.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): The two effects stack, granting 6 stacks for each enemy champion taken down by an ability, 7 with Baleful Strike.

basic_ability_1.name: Baleful Strike
basic_ability_1.description_source: Active:  Veigar blasts a dark bolt in the target direction that deals magic damage to the first two enemies hit.

effects.context_notes: Active: Veigar blasts a dark bolt in the target direction that deals magic damage to the first two enemies hit.

effects.description: If this kills an enemy, it generates 1 stack of Phenomenal Evil, tripled against large minions and monsters.

basic_ability_1.description: Active: Veigar blasts a dark bolt in the target direction that deals magic damage to the first two enemies hit.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): Killing two enemy champions with Baleful Strike grants +14 AP.

basic_ability_2.name: Dark Matter
basic_ability_2.description_source: Active:  Veigar casts down a mass of dark matter that strikes the target location after a brief delay, dealing magic damage to enemies hit.

effects.context_notes: Active: Veigar casts down a mass of dark matter that strikes the target location after a 1.

effects.description: Dark Matter's cooldown is reduced based on stacks of Phenomenal Evil.

basic_ability_2.description: Active: Veigar casts down a mass of dark matter that strikes the target location after a 1.221 seconds delay, dealing magic damage to enemies hit.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): The delay starts at the beginning of the cast time.

basic_ability_3.name: Event Horizon
basic_ability_3.description_source: Active: After a brief delay,  Veigar erects a cage at the target location that lasts for a short time. Enemies that touch the cage edges are briefly  knocked down and  stunned.

effects.context_notes: Active: Veigar forms a cage at the target location that erects after a 0.

effects.description: Event Horizon can affect enemies only once per cast.

basic_ability_3.description: Active: Veigar forms a cage at the target location that erects after a 0.5 second delay, remaining there for 3 seconds. Enemies that collide with the edges of the cage are knocked down and stunned for a duration.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): The stun and the knockdown are applied to units that are within a certain distance interval from the center of the area of effect.

Manual verification (2026-02-24): Event Horizon creates a delayed cage where only edge contact applies knockdown plus stun; crowd-control timing is defined by edge touch and cage lifetime rather than immediate center impact.

ultimate.name: Primordial Burst
ultimate.description_source: Active:  Veigar sends a primordial burst at the target enemy champion that deals magic damage based on their missing health.

effects.context_notes: Active: Veigar sends a primordial burst at the target enemy champion that deals magic damage, increased by 0% : 100% (based on target's missing health).
Active: Veigar sends a primordial burst at the target enemy champion that deals magic damage, increased by 0% : 100% (based on target's missing health).

ultimate.description: Active: Veigar sends a primordial burst at the target enemy champion that deals magic damage, increased by 0% : 100% (based on target's missing health).
context_notes: Source notes (summary): If the target becomes untargetable, dies, or is too far away or no longer in sight during the cast time, this ability will cancel but does not go on cooldown nor pay its cost (if applicable).

sources.used_for: canonical champion baseline generation for stats, abilities, and execution metadata

sources.type: League of Legends Wiki (ability data template)
sources.used_for: manual behavior verification for Event Horizon execution timing, activation gating, and player-visible resolution semantics

notes: Generated from From Online champion payload as part of champion corpus parity coverage.
Ability execution metadata is source-derived from castTime/speed/effectRadius when present.
This baseline should be manually refined for high-impact simulation nuances before runtime scripting expansion.

schema_notes.base_by_rank: Arrays indexed by ability rank (1-based in game, 0-based in data).
schema_notes.base_by_champion_level: Arrays indexed by champion level minus one.
schema_notes.formula: Formula objects use explicit op/terms/output_stat when reliable source math is available.
schema_notes.ratio_type: Common ratio types: total_ad, bonus_ad, ap, target_max_health, target_missing_health.
schema_notes.value_percent: Percent values are represented as whole percentages unless noted otherwise.
schema_notes.basic_attack: Basic attack timing and projectile defaults are source-derived with documented fallbacks.
schema_notes.basic_attack_raw_timing_stats: Raw timing stats preserve source payload fields for auditability.
